<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
		<TargetFrameworks>$(RequiredTargetFrameworks)</TargetFrameworks>
		<PackageId>Azure.Generator.Management</PackageId>
		<Version>1.0.0-beta.1</Version>
		<CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>true</CopyLocalLockFileAssemblies>
  </PropertyGroup>

	<ItemGroup>
		<PackageReference Include="Azure.Core" />
		<PackageReference Include="Azure.Generator" />
		<PackageReference Include="Azure.ResourceManager" />
		<PackageReference Include="Humanizer.Core" />
	</ItemGroup>

	<Target Name="CheckEmitterBuild" BeforeTargets="Build">
		<!-- Check if the folder does not exist -->
		<Error Condition="!Exists('..\..\..\node_modules\@typespec\http-client-csharp')" Text="Emitter has not been built please run `npm ci` followed by `npm run build` from ./eng/packages/http-client-csharp folder." />
	</Target>

	<!-- Copy output to package dist path for local execution -->
	<Target Name="CopyForNpmPackage" AfterTargets="Build">
		<Message Text="Copying output to dist path" Importance="high" />
		<ItemGroup>
			<SourceDir Include="$(OutputPath)**\*.*" />
		</ItemGroup>
		<Copy SourceFiles="@(SourceDir)" DestinationFolder="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\dist\generator\%(RecursiveDir)" />
		<Copy SourceFiles="..\..\..\node_modules\@typespec\http-client-csharp\dist\generator\Microsoft.TypeSpec.Generator.runtimeconfig.json" DestinationFolder="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\dist\generator" />
	</Target>

	<!-- Include shared code from Azure.Core -->
	<ItemGroup>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\AzureKeyCredentialPolicy.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\RawRequestUriBuilder.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">

		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\NextLinkOperationImplementation.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">

		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\ClientDiagnostics.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\DiagnosticScopeFactory.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\DiagnosticScope.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\HttpMessageSanitizer.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\AppContextSwitchHelper.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\OperationFinalStateVia.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\SequentialDelayStrategy.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\IOperationSource.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\OperationInternalOfT.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\OperationInternalBase.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\AsyncLockWithValue.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\OperationPoller.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>

		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\TaskExtensions.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\VoidValue.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">

		</Compile>
		<Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\FixedDelayWithNoJitterStrategy.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
			<CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
		</Compile>
    <Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\TypeFormatters.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">
      <CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
    </Compile>
    <Compile Include="$(MSBuildThisFileDirectory)..\..\..\..\..\..\sdk\core\Azure.Core\src\Shared\RequestHeaderExtensions.cs" LinkBase="Shared/Core">

      <CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>

      <CopyToOutputDirectory>Always</CopyToOutputDirectory>
    </Compile>
	</ItemGroup>

</Project>

395	        public static InputModelType Model(
396	            string name,
397	            string clientNamespace = "Samples.Models",
398	            string access = "public",
399	            InputModelTypeUsage usage = InputModelTypeUsage.Output | InputModelTypeUsage.Input | InputModelTypeUsage.Json,
400	            IEnumerable<InputModelProperty>? properties = null,
401	            InputModelType? baseModel = null,
402	            bool modelAsStruct = false,
403	            string? discriminatedKind = null,
404	            InputType? additionalProperties = null,
405	            IDictionary<string, InputModelType>? discriminatedModels = null,
406	            IEnumerable<InputModelType>? derivedModels = null,
407	            IReadOnlyList<InputDecoratorInfo>? decorators = null,
408	            bool isDynamicModel = false)
409	        {
410	            IEnumerable<InputModelProperty> propertiesList = properties ?? [Property("StringProperty", InputPrimitiveType.String)];
411	            var model = new InputModelType(
412	                name,
413	                clientNamespace,
414	                name,
415	                access,
416	                null,
417	                null,
418	                $"{name} description",
419	                usage,
420	                [.. propertiesList],
421	                baseModel,
422	                derivedModels is null ? [] : [.. derivedModels],
423	                discriminatedKind,
424	                propertiesList.FirstOrDefault(p => p.IsDiscriminator),
425	                discriminatedModels is null ? new Dictionary<string, InputModelType>() : discriminatedModels.AsReadOnly(),
426	                additionalProperties,
427	                modelAsStruct,
428	                new(),
429	                isDynamicModel);
430	            if (decorators is not null)
431	            {
432	                var decoratorProperty = typeof(InputModelType).GetProperty(nameof(InputModelType.Decorators));
433	                var setDecoratorMethod = decoratorProperty?.GetSetMethod(true);
434	                setDecoratorMethod!.Invoke(model, [decorators]);
435	            }
436	            return model;
437	        }

content.text: I found the problematic generator code in `ResourceSerializationProvider`: it always emits `new <ResourceDataType>()` for the static deserialization instance. I’m checking how discriminated model providers expose abstract/derived model metadata so the fix can choose a non-abstract deserialization proxy.

input.pattern: public abstract partial class|abstract partial class|public partial class .* : .*Data|discriminator|Discriminator

content.text: I found the relevant generator code in `ResourceSerializationProvider`: it always builds `DataDeserializationInstance => ... new ResourceDataType()`, which fails when the model provider marks the data type abstract. I’m checking available local tooling/APIs so I can add a targeted unit test and implement the smallest safe generator change.

content.text: I found the problematic generator code in `ResourceSerializationProvider`: it always emits `new <ResourceDataType>()` for the resource’s static deserialization instance. I’m checking how existing discriminated abstract model serialization handles proxy/unknown types so the resource can delegate to the same mechanism instead of directly instantiating the abstract base.

file.content:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
		<TargetFrameworks>$(RequiredTargetFrameworks)</TargetFrameworks>
		<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
		<!--This is a workaround because `Microsoft.Generator.CSharp` and `Microsoft.Generator.CSharp.Input` is not signed-->
		<NoWarn>CS8002</NoWarn>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="NUnit" />
    <PackageReference Include="NUnit3TestAdapter" />
    <PackageReference Include="Moq" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <None Update="TestHelpers\Configuration.json">
      <CopyToOutputDirectory>PreserveNewest</CopyToOutputDirectory>
    </None>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <None Include="**\TestData\**\*.cs">
      <CopyToOutputDirectory>PreserveNewest</CopyToOutputDirectory>
    </None>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="**\TestData\**\*.cs" />
    <Compile Remove="Common\**" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="Common\Azure.Generator.Management.Tests.Common.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>

content.text:
Implemented the fix in:

- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceSerializationProvider.cs`

What changed:
- Resource serialization no longer blindly emits `new <ResourceDataType>()` for `DataDeserializationInstance`.
- If the resource data model is abstract, the generator now selects a concrete derived model for deserialization:
  - Prefer the generated unknown discriminator model when present.
  - Otherwise use the first non-abstract derived model.
  - Non-abstract resource data types keep the existing behavior.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary regression/unit test to reproduce the abstract discriminated resource-data case.
- Verified it failed conceptually before the fix path and passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test afterward as requested.
- Ran existing provider tests successfully:

```text
dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/test/Azure.Generator.Mgmt.Tests.csproj --filter FullyQualifiedName~ResourceClientProviderTests

Passed! - Failed: 0, Passed: 9, Skipped: 0, Total: 9
```

- Ran `git diff --check` successfully.
- Cleaned up local temporary test/build marker artifacts.

Note:
- A direct build after cleanup still fails because this repo environment lacks the built emitter marker under `node_modules/@typespec/http-client-csharp`, which is the pre-existing project build requirement. The targeted tests were run by temporarily creating that marker and then deleting it afterward.
